stylePrompt: Create a completely original lighting product inspired by Light French elegance. The design should feel romantic, soft, graceful, refined, feminine but not childish, luxurious but not heavy. Avoid overly ornate classical French reproduction. Avoid heavy palace style. Avoid excessive carving and gold decoration. The final product should reinterpret French elegance in a contemporary, light, airy, and commercially viable way.
specificElements: The soft pleat of a ballet tutu
The curved profile of antique French mirror frames
The petal-like edge of French porcelain
The gentle ruffle of draped linen
The bead rhythm of vintage pearl jewelry
The airy curve of Art Nouveau ironwork
The soft scalloped edge of lace
The delicate curve of a French garden chair
The fluted rhythm of palace molding
metalTranslationPrompt: Translate light French elegance into metal through slim champagne-brass frames, softly fluted sheet-metal shades, restrained scroll curves, delicate but manufacturable floral terminals, and pale ivory powder coating.
